data.title: Theratechnologies issues update on Egrifta SV shortage
data.description:
Theratechnologies (NASDAQ:THTX) said there will be a shortage of its drug Egrifta SV on the patient level in mid-January due to the voluntary shutdown of a contract manufacturing facility last year.

The Canadian company added that several U.S. pharmacies have reported stockouts of the drug.

Theratechnologies (NASDAQ:THTX) filed a Prior Approval Supplement with the FDA last month and has submitted questions about the status of two recently manufactured batches of the drug that are waiting to be released to pharmacies.

The FDA confirmed that it would respond to the company’s questions in writing no later than February 8, according to a company statement.

Egrifta SV is used to reduce excess abdominal fat in adults with HIV and lipodystrophy.

Theratechnologies (THTX) said in December that production of the drug had resumed following a voluntary shutdown of the contract manufacturing facility to address observations from an FDA inspection.

data.title: NOVONIX, Harper International sign licensing agreement for graphitization furnace technology
data.description:
• NOVONIX (NASDAQ:NVX) on Thursday said it has entered into a licensing agreement with Harper International Corporation, for the rights and use of its continuous, induction-based graphitization furnace technology.
  • Alongside this exclusive use agreement, the licensing agreement provides NOVONIX the right to an exclusive license to Harper’s technology, on which its continuous graphitization furnaces operate.
  • Upon making an initial payment within 12 months of the effective date of the agreement, NOVONIX will have the exclusive license to use the technology to further develop the furnaces used for the thermal production of graphite material for use in the battery anode market.
  • NOVONIX must make an initial payment no later than December 2025 to gain a license to design and develop the graphitization furnace technology for its internal use, said the company.
  • At the expiration of this seven-year period, the company will have a perpetual, irrevocable, and non-exclusive license to the technology for use only by NOVONIX or any sublicensee approved by Harper, it added.
  • NVNXF closed -8.0% at $0.46.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pollard Banknote renews secured revolving credit facility
data.description:
• Pollard Banknote (TSX:PBL:CA) on Thursday said the company and its syndicate of lenders have executed a renewal of its secured revolving credit facility.
  • As per the renewal, the term has been extended by three years, maturing on December 31, 2028, said the company.
  • The facility size has been increased by $50 million to $291 million, with an accordion feature which can increase the facility by an additional $50 million, it added.
  • The company said the facility will be available for general working capital purposes, ongoing investments in capital expansion.
  • PBL:CA closed -1.05% at $24.27
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canada Nickel Co extends repayment date of $15M loan facility
data.description:
• Canada Nickel Company (TSXV:CNC:CA) said on Thursday that it has extended the repayment date of the US$15 million loan facility announced July 9, 2024 with Auramet International, from January 9, 2025, to March 14, 2025.
  • An extension fee of US$438,465 will be paid by March 14, 2025 and 1,750,000 warrants with a strike price of $0.96 per share will be issued with a 9 month expiry. 750,000 warrants previously issued with the initial loan will be cancelled.
  • The loan will carry an interest rate of 1.25% per month for the extension period. The interest payable on the original loan amount has also been deferred to March 14th, 2025.

data.title: Inovalis Real Estate Investment Trust announces sale of 87.5% of the Arcueil Property for €37.5M
data.description:
Inovalis Real Estate Investment Trust (INO.UN:CA) announced on Thursday the signing of an exchange contract for the sale of 87.5% of the Arcueil property to two parties for €37.5 million ($56.5 million) which reflects, on a pro-rated basis, 100% of the property’s appraised value of €45.0 million ($67.8 million).

The sale is conditional upon the satisfaction of certain conditions including the issuance of a building permit, the receipt of financing by the purchasers as well as other municipal approval and environmental conditions.

The expected net proceeds from this transaction after the full repayment of bank debt related to the property is expected to be approximately €6.2 million ($9.3 million) and would be used for capital expenditures relating to the re-positioning and/or re-development of currently owned properties and further reducing the REIT’s indebtedness.

The closing of the transaction is only expected to take place at the earliest in the second half of 2026. The remaining 12.5% of the property is being marketed separately.

data.title: Civeo receives 6-year contract extension in Australia
data.description:
• Civeo Corporation (NYSE:CVEO) on Thursday said it was awarded a six-year contract extension with a resources player to provide integrated services at eleven villages in Western Australia, effective January 1, 2025.
  • The contract expands the company’s scope from operating seven villages to eleven villages.
  • The new contract will incorporate catering and retail services; village, mine and port site cleaning services; facilities maintenance; and the provision of health and wellbeing solutions, said the company.
  • It is anticipated the six-year contract will generate approximately A$1.4B in revenues over the 2025-2030 contract period, it added.
  • CVEO -1.67% after hours to $22.83.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Intellia to cut 27% of workforce, discontinue NTLA-3001
data.description:
Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA) said it plans to focus its resources on developing drug candidates NTLA-2002 and nex-z and discontinue development of NTLA-3001, which will result in the company cutting around 27% of its workforce.

The biotech company said it sees NTLA-2002 and nex-z as having "the greatest opportunity to create significant, near-term value.” NTLA-2002 is being developed for hereditary angioedema and nex-z for ATTR amyloidosis.

NTLA-3001 was being developed for the treatment of alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency-associated lung disease. The company is also discontinuing certain research-stage programs.

Intellia (NASDAQ:NTLA) said it expects to incur charges of around $8M in Q1 2025 related to the restructuring. The company ended 2024 with approximately $862M in cash, equivalents and investments, which should be able to fund operations into the first half of 2027, according to a statement.

The company also announced that its chief scientific officer, Laura Sepp-Lorenzino, was retiring. She will continue with the company as a senior scientific advisor through the remainder of the year. Intellia's (NASDAQ:NTLA) head of immunology and cell therapy, Birgit Schultes, will assume the post of CSO on Jan. 13.

Intellia (NTLA) added that it plans to complete the buildout of its commercial leadership team by the second half of 2025.

data.title: Eni poised for €3B in asset sales this year, UBS says in downgrade
data.description:
Eni's (NYSE:E) asset selloff plan is more than halfway completed and is far ahead of schedule, UBS analysts said Thursday, adding that the remaining disposals this year likely will be focused primarily on the upstream business.

Eni's (NYSE:E) strategic plan calls for selling ~€8B in assets through 2027, with nearly half coming from sales of stakes in company units and the other half from upstream activities, and UBS analysts led by Joshua Stone said the company already has sold assets totaling €5.9B (~$6.1B), while another €3B of sales should be announced by the end of 2025.

But UBS downgraded Eni (E) shares to Neutral from Buy with a €14 price target, down from the previous €15.50 PT, saying the company's rapid advancements have led to a situation where there is "less room for positive surprise" in earnings and the scope of buybacks for 2025.

"We see a risk that Eni becomes a victim of its own success," the analysts wrote. "Given the fast progress made, we think most of the good news is priced in, and the risk is slightly skewed to the downside should there be any delays."

The bank also noted Eni (E) shares currently trade at a 5% premium to their historical enterprise value to debt-adjusted cash flow ratio, suggesting limited potential for further appreciation.

data.title: Brookdale Senior Living occupancy increases to 79.3% in December
data.description:
• Brookdale Senior Living (NYSE:BKD) on Thursday reported that its weighted average occupancy increased 100 basis points year-over-year, from 78.3% in 2023 to 79.3% in December 2024.
  • Fourth quarter 2024 weighted average occupancy also increased 100 basis points compared to the prior year quarter.
  • Fourth quarter 2024 move-in volume surpassed the prior year and exceeded the pre-pandemic average for this period by 8%.
  • BKD -2.86% after hours to $4.74.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ImmuCell reports higher prelim Q4 sales
data.description:
• ImmuCell Corporation (NASDAQ:ICCC) announced preliminary sales of $7.8M in Q4, a 52% increase from Q4 2023.
  • Co sees FY 2024 sales of $26.5M, also a 52% increase from 2023.
  • The company believes that the current backlog reflects strong demand as distributors work to replenish their buffer stocks and as end-users place orders in advance of peak calving season during the first quarter.

  • Source: ImmuCell Corporation
